title: Urce

'''Urce''' was a [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Lesser Plooriod Cluster/Legends|Lesser Plooriod Cluster]]. A member of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] since at least the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] Urce served the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] as a regional seat of power and was the site of the [[Mox Slosin's palace|palace]] of [[High Inquisitor]] [[Mox Slosin]]. In [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]], the [[Imperial Remnant]] was victorious over the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] at a [[Battle of Urce|confrontation]] at Urce.

==Description==
Urce was a terrestrial<ref name="CSWE Slosin" /> [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="CSWE Urce" /> located in the [[Urce system/Legends|Urce system]].<ref name="TEA Urce" /> It was a part of [[Urce sector/Legends|Urce Space]], which was located in the [[Lesser Plooriod Cluster/Legends|Lesser Plooriod Cluster]]<ref name="CSWE Urce" /> and constituted a part of the [[New Territories/Legends|New Territories]] portion of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="TEA Urce" /> Between approximately [[12,000 BBY/Legends|12,000 BBY]] and [[11,000 BBY]], Urce was still considered a part of the ill-[[Scout/Legends|explored]] [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]].<ref name="EGTW Urce" />

==History==
[[File:Mox_Slosin.jpg|thumb|left|220px|Urce served as a base of operations for Imperial High Inquisitor Mox Slosin.]]
In [[11,884 BBY]], the area of Wild Space in which Urce was situated was targeted by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] when it carried out its [[Great Northern Crusade]] against [[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorians]], [[Zabrak/Legends|Zabraks]], and other [[Species/Legends|species]] inhabiting the region.<ref name="EGTW Urce" /> [[Realspace/Legends|Space]] surrounding Urce was only explored between [[3000 BBY]] and [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]]. By the [[Time/Legends|time]] the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] of [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]–[[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] were fought, Urce was situated within Republic borders,<ref name="TEA Urce" /> and the Republic's [[Ninth Army|Ninth Sector Army]] was charged with engaging the [[Confederacy military/Legends|military forces]] of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] in the planet's vicinity during the first campaigns of that conflict.<ref name="EGTW Urce" />

Urce was situated within the territory controlled by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] by [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]].<ref name="EGTW Urce" /> At some point following the successful Imperial [[Subjugation of Fitca|subjugation]]<ref name="AIR">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]''</ref> of the [[Fitca system]]<ref name="CSWE Fitca">{{CSWECite|I|277|Fitca Prime}}</ref> in Urce Space<ref name="EAOC Fitca">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Fitca system]]</ref> by [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]],<ref name="Date">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'' represents a collection of [[in-universe]] reports from "one [[Standard year/Legends|year]] before the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]," which corresponds to [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> the Imperial [[High Inquisitor]] [[Mox Slosin]] was transferred from the Fitca [[Star system/Legends|system]] to Urce.<ref name="AIR" /> By [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]], Urce was a part of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. That [[Standard year/Legends|year]], [[Imperial Military/Legends|forces]] of the [[Imperial Remnant]] were victorious over the New Republic at a [[Battle of Urce|confrontation]] there.<ref name="EGTW Urce" /> As part of their [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]] of the [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]], at some point by [[26 ABY/Legends|26 ABY]], the [[Yuuzhan Vong galaxy|extra-galactic]] [[Yuuzhan Vong]] gained control over the Urce system, and it remained the invaders' possession by [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]].<ref name="TEA Urce" />

==Inhabitants==
Urce was the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of its surrounding space during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] and served as a seat of Imperial power at the time.<ref name="CSWE Urce" /> Around [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], the population of the Urce system numbered between one and ten billion.<ref name="TEA Urce" />

==Locations==
During the Galactic Civil War, Urce was the site of a [[Mox Slosin's palace|palace]] used by the Imperial High Inquisitor Mox Slosin. The [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitor]] terrorized the Lesser Plooriod Cluster from the palace but also spent much of his time<ref name="CSWE Slosin" /> actively traveling along the [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlanes]] of the [[Star cluster/Legends|cluster]] aboard his [[Trenchant-class cruiser|light cruiser]], the [[Ironhand (Trenchant-class)|''Ironhand'']], in search of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] cells operating in the area.<ref name="AIR" /> By 12 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]], a [[HoloNet relay/Legends|HoloNet relay]] was also situated at Urce.<ref name="EGTW Urce" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Allianceintelligencereports.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Urce was introduced in ''Alliance Intelligence Reports''.]]
Urce was first mentioned in ''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'', a [[1995]] sourcebook for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="AIR" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Urce system, and therefore the planet itself, in grid square L-6.<ref name="TEA Urce" />

The entry for Urce in the [[2008]] ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' introduced an apparent contradiction regarding the planet's status. The entry claims that Urce was "the seat of Imperial power at the height of the New Order,"<ref name="CSWE Urce" /> which contrasts with other ''[[Star Wars]]'' sources agreeing that the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] planet [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] was instead the capital of the Galactic Empire at that time. This article therefore treats the use of the definite article "the" in ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'' entry as erroneous.<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref><ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref><ref name="EGTW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>
